paper_metadata.title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations
paper_metadata.abstract: Increasing model size when pretraining natural language representations often results in improved performance on downstream tasks. However, at some point further model increases become harder due to GPU/TPU memory limitations and longer training times. To address these problems, we present two parameter-reduction techniques to lower memory consumption and increase the training speed of BERT. Comprehensive empirical evidence shows that our proposed methods lead to models that scale much better compared to the original BERT. We also use a self-supervised loss that focuses on modeling inter-sentence coherence, and show it consistently helps downstream tasks with multi-sentence inputs. As a result, our best model establishes new state-of-the-art results on the GLUE, RACE, and \squad benchmarks while having fewer parameters compared to BERT-large. The code and the pretrained models are available at https://github.com/google-research/ALBERT.

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces factorized embedding parameterization that separates the large vocabulary embedding matrix into two smaller matrices, and cross-layer parameter sharing that significantly reduces parameter count while maintaining performance. It also replaces Next Sentence Prediction with Sentence Order Prediction to focus on discourse-level coherence.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM), Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.extension: Factorized embedding parameterization separates the embedding matrix into two smaller matrices (E × H instead of V × H) to reduce parameters while maintaining representational capacity.
extraction_data.research_problem: Parameter efficiency in language representation models
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, question answering, reading comprehension
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces factorized embedding parameterization that separates the large vocabulary embedding matrix into two smaller matrices, and cross-layer parameter sharing that significantly reduces parameter count while maintaining performance. It also replaces Next Sentence Prediction with Sentence Order Prediction to focus on discourse-level coherence.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, question answering, reading comprehension
extraction_data.research_problem: Parameter efficiency in language representation models
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM), Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.extension: Sentence Order Prediction (SOP) loss focuses on modeling inter-sentence coherence by using positive examples of consecutive segments and negative examples with swapped order, avoiding topic prediction.
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces factorized embedding parameterization that separates the large vocabulary embedding matrix into two smaller matrices, and cross-layer parameter sharing that significantly reduces parameter count while maintaining performance. extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked Language Modeling (MLM), Sentence Order Prediction (SOP)
extraction_data.extension: ALBERT-xxlarge uses a 12-layer network instead of 24-layer to maintain computational efficiency while achieving high performance with 235M parameters.
